zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Dalyson於1523年出生在英國貝德福爾凱時，她的父親George Dalyson27歲，母親Jane Lenthroppe23歲。她和John Leventhorpe至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1551年在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年28歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Dalyson于1523年出生在英国貝德福爾凱时，她的父亲George Dalyson27岁，母亲Jane Lenthroppe23岁。她和John Leventhorpe至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1551年在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年28岁，埋葬在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Dalyson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: patronymic or metronymic meaning ‘son of the dairyman or dairywoman’ (from Middle English day(e), dey(e) + son).
variant of Dawson with the original diphthong /au/ simplified to /a:/ in late Middle English and subsequently pronounced /εɪ/. Compare Dakin for Dawkin (see Dawkins ).

variants.name: Dawson
meanings: English: patronymic meaning ‘son of Dawe’, from the Middle English personal name Daw(e) (see Daw 1 and 2), which is sometimes a pet form of David (see 2 below) but more generally of Rauf or Raw (i.e. Ralph ), of which Daw(e) is a rhyming form. Alternatively, the patronymic could mean ‘son of Daud(e)’, another Middle English pet form of Ralph evidently used interchangeably with Dawe. Dawson is one of the most numerous patronymics.
Scottish: patronymic, ‘son of Dawe’. In Scotland Dawe is a diminutive of David (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Deason
meanings: English: variant of   Dayson   in either sense.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Norwegian Disen (see Dyson 2).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Dalyson was born in 1523, in Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England, her father, George Dalyson, was 27 and her mother, Jane Lenthroppe, was 23. She had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with John Leventhorpe. She died in 1551, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 28, and was buried in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Dalyson est née en 1523, à Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, George Dalyson, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Jane Lenthroppe, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec John Leventhorpe. Elle est décédée en 1551, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterrée à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Dalyson 1523 in Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater George Dalyson 27 und ihre Mutter Jane Lenthroppe 23. Sie hatte mit John Leventhorpe mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1551 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England und wurde in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Dalyson è nata il nel 1523 nel Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England, sua padre, George Dalyson, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Jane Lenthroppe, aveva 23 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con John Leventhorpe. Morì nel 1551, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 28 anni, e fu sepolta a Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Dalysonが1523年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアケイソで生まれた時，父George Dalysonは27歳，母Jane Lenthroppeは23歳であった。彼女はJohn Leventhorpeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1551年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで28歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Dalyson(이)가 1523년 영국 베드퍼드실르 케이소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Dalyson(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Lenthroppe(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 John Leventhorpe(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1551년 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Dalyson nasceu em 1523, em Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, George Dalyson, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Jane Lenthroppe, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com John Leventhorpe. Ela faleceu em 1551, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 28 anos, e foi sepultada em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Dalyson родилась в 1523 г. в местности кисо, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени George Dalyson было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Lenthroppe, 23. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от John Leventhorpe. Она умерла в 1551 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 28 лет и была похоронена в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Dalyson nació en 1523, en Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, George Dalyson, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Jane Lenthroppe, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con John Leventhorpe. Murió en 1551, en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultada en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
